reasoning: Initial observation shows d1=10, d2=20, d3=30 and total_distance=60. The sum of the segments (10+20+30) equals the total distance (60). This suggests the equation might be total_distance = d1 + d2 + d3. To confirm this hypothesis, I will perturb one variable, specifically increasing d1, and observe if total_distance increases by the same amount.

I have observed that increasing d1 by 10 resulted in total_distance increasing by 10 (from 60 to 70), while d2 and d3 remained constant. This linear relationship supports the hypothesis that total_distance is the sum of d1, d2, and d3. To be thorough, I will perturb d2 next to confirm the additive nature of the other variables.

I have confirmed that increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, and increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. This strongly suggests an additive relationship. To be absolutely certain, I will perturb d3 next to verify that it also contributes linearly to the total distance.

I have observed that increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10, and increasing d3 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. In all cases, the change in total_distance equals the change in the perturbed variable while others remain constant. This confirms the linear additive relationship: total_distance = d1 + d2 + d3. I have gathered sufficient evidence to conclude the equation.
